Abbreviations: FPL, Federal Poverty Level; GLP-1RA, glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist; MEPS, Medical Expenditure Panel Survey; SGLT2i, sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitor.


Population: Medicare beneficiaries aged 65 years or older in MEPS 2022.


Columns compare beneficiaries with any observed Medicare Part D prescription drug coverage during 2022 with beneficiaries who had Medicare but no observed Part D coverage during the MEPS reference periods.


Estimates use the full-year MEPS person weight because this table describes the wider Medicare population rather than the BMI-defined policy cohort.


Clinical Proxies includes the MEPS-observable conditions used to approximate CMS Medicare GLP-1 Bridge BMI/comorbidity pathways and Part D-coverable GLP-1 indications. Heart failure is any observable heart failure, not confirmed heart failure with preserved ejection fraction. Chronic kidney disease is observable CKD, not confirmed stage 3a or higher. Peripheral artery disease is observable PAD, not confirmed symptomatic PAD.


Type 2 diabetes, obstructive sleep apnea, and MASH/liver disease are shown as neutral baseline characteristics in this table. In policy simulations, they are used as observable proxies for Part D-coverable GLP-1 indications that route beneficiaries away from Bridge pricing.


Income categories are based on the Federal Poverty Level.


Costs, where shown, are reported in January 2026 US dollars.

Population: Medicare Part D beneficiaries aged 65 years or older with an observable adult BMI and observable CMS GLP-1 Bridge BMI/comorbidity pathway criteria in MEPS 2022.


Rows show the overall analytic population and mutually exclusive BMI categories corresponding to the CMS Bridge BMI ranges. The BMI rows are defined by BMI alone: BMI >=35, BMI 30-34.9, and BMI 27-29.9.


Estimates use the MEPS self-administered questionnaire weight because Bridge pathway eligibility depends on the adult BMI measure collected in that questionnaire.


Medicare Part D coverage means prescription drug benefit coverage observed at any point during the 2022 MEPS reference periods.


Baseline reflects observed annual OOP health care spending. Universal reflects simulated full-year GLP-1RA therapy for the analytic cohort plus targeted full-year SGLT2i therapy only among beneficiaries with type 2 diabetes, heart failure, or chronic kidney disease.


† The dagger applies to the three columns under each policy scenario. Revised OOP is estimated for the population in the row shown. Benefiting, % is the percentage of beneficiaries in that row whose OOP spending is lower than under Universal therapy. Beneficiary Savings is estimated only among beneficiaries in that row who benefit under that scenario, not among the full row population.


Standalone policy scenarios are mutually exclusive. Cost Plus SGLT2i caps targeted SGLT2i OOP at $120/year where applicable. Medicare Bridge caps GLP-1RA OOP at $600/year only among Bridge-eligible beneficiaries. IRA caps regular Part D prescription-drug OOP at $2,100/year without Bridge pricing.


The combined scenario applies Cost Plus SGLT2i and then assigns each person the lower-cost GLP-1RA route: Bridge pricing when eligible versus regular Part D coverage subject to the IRA cap. A person cannot receive GLP-1RA through both Bridge and IRA pathways simultaneously.


Bridge exclusion proxies for type 2 diabetes, sleep apnea, and MASH/liver disease use observable MEPS public-use condition information and are not exact clinical adjudications.


Costs are reported in January 2026 US dollars. Confidence intervals for costs use bootstrap-estimated standard errors with 2,000 replicates and log transformation.


Estimates marked with an asterisk are statistically unreliable by 2017 National Center for Health Statistics standards.

Table: annualized GLP-1RA OOP cost distributions in Medicare, shown as weighted population size, median, IQR, and 10th-90th percentile range.


Donor pool: observed Medicare beneficiaries aged 65 years or older with valid observed adult BMI using GLP-1RA therapy with nonmissing daily OOP costs. Donors may or may not have observed Part D coverage, because the imputation donor pool is defined by the Medicare insurance stratum.


Predictive mean matching used random forests with age, continuous adult BMI, sex, race and ethnicity, census region, family income, federal poverty level percentage, background prescription-drug OOP spending, premium OOP spending, inpatient OOP spending, outpatient/office-based OOP spending, observed Part D status, and the Clinical Proxies listed in Table 1a as predictors.


Observed recipient users: Medicare Part D beneficiaries aged 65 years or older in the policy recipient cohort who were already using GLP-1RA therapy.


Simulated recipients: Medicare Part D beneficiaries aged 65 years or older in the Bridge-pathway analytic cohort after assigning observed or imputed GLP-1RA daily costs.


Costs are annualized by multiplying daily OOP cost by 365 and are reported in January 2026 US dollars.
